project.name: sdd-pipeline
project.description: Semantic search pipeline that converts Confluence-exported markdown into a vector-search index for Software Design Documents, plus an HTML to GitLab-Markdown converter.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the sdd_pipeline package; re-exports the public API (PipelineConfig, the data models, and SemanticPipeline) so callers can import from the top-level package.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package __init__ acting as a barrel; aggregates exports from config, models, and pipeline modules.

nodes.name: chunking.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic stage that turns a DocumentModel section tree into a list of SemanticChunks, handling prose/definition merging, embed-budget-aware text splitting, and per-chunk entity scoping.
nodes.languageNotes: Recursive section walk with nested closures (emit/flush) for buffering prose merges.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Defines PipelineConfig, the pydantic-settings configuration model that loads all pipeline settings from PIPELINE_-prefixed env vars or a .env file, with a pydantic-v1 fallback branch.
nodes.languageNotes: Dual-branch pydantic-settings v2 / pydantic-v1 fallback; new config fields must be added to both branches.

nodes.name: embeddings.py
nodes.summary: The only module that loads an embedding backend: provides EmbedderProtocol plus local sentence-transformers and Azure OpenAI embedders, selected by make_embedder(config).
nodes.languageNotes: Protocol-based pluggable backends with lazy model loading; optional openai SDK imported only inside the Azure path.

nodes.name: extract_prose.py
nodes.summary: Extracts candidate entity terms from prose sections using regex heuristics (all-caps, backtick, PascalCase, kebab, paths) and optional spaCy noun chunks, producing a prose inventory for enrichment.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Pure data contracts for the pipeline: ContentBlock/Section/DocumentModel structural types, the SemanticChunk record with its embed-text/metadata/dict serializers, enums (ContentType, SectionType), and EntityRecord.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen/dataclass data contracts with no service logic; SemanticChunk.to_embed_text builds the structured embedding header.

nodes.name: pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the 7-stage indexing/search flow via SemanticPipeline, lazily wiring the embedder and vector store, enforcing the chunk-quality gate, verifying index provenance, and running dense plus hybrid (RRF) search.
nodes.languageNotes: Lazy property-based dependency injection so unit tests can mock the embedder and vector store.

nodes.name: quality.py
nodes.summary: Stdlib-only quality gates: check_markdown lints raw source markdown for embedding-harmful residue, and check_chunk is the binding structural-absence hygiene gate run on produced chunk embed-text.
nodes.languageNotes: De-fences code (blanking fenced/inline code while preserving line numbers) before running prose residue checks to avoid false positives on code examples.

nodes.name: tui.py
nodes.summary: Interactive Textual-based search TUI (SearchApp) layered over SemanticPipeline that keeps the embedder and index warm across queries, with live query box, section/space filters, and a hybrid toggle.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports textual only when the command runs; blocking pipeline.search dispatched via asyncio.to_thread to keep the UI responsive.

nodes.name: vector_store.py
nodes.summary: The only module touching vector-store backends: defines VectorStoreProtocol with pluggable in-memory (langchain-core) and Chroma implementations, plus SearchResult, selected by make_vector_store(config).
nodes.languageNotes: Both backend libraries imported lazily; memory backend persists via atomic tmp+replace writes with a precomputed-embeddings stub that refuses re-embedding.

nodes.name: vocabulary.py
nodes.summary: JSON load/save helpers for the cross-corpus entity vocabulary, keeping vocabulary file I/O out of enrichment.py.

nodes.name: _split_text
nodes.summary: Splits a block of text into chunks under max_chars, preferring paragraph boundaries and falling back to sentence boundaries to stay within the embed budget.

nodes.name: _header_reserve
nodes.summary: Estimates the character cost of the structural header prepended to a chunk's embed text so the content split width leaves room for it.

nodes.name: _section_to_chunks
nodes.summary: Recursively converts a Section (and its subsections) into SemanticChunks, applying prose/definition merging, signal filtering, table separation, and per-chunk entity recomputation.

nodes.name: chunk_document
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that walks a DocumentModel's root sections and aggregates them into the full list of SemanticChunks for the document.

nodes.name: make_embedder
nodes.summary: Factory that selects and constructs the embedder backend (local sentence-transformers or Azure OpenAI) based on config.embedding_provider.

nodes.name: embedder_identity
nodes.summary: Returns the (provider, model, dimension) identity tuple used as index provenance to detect incompatible vector spaces.

nodes.name: EmbeddingModel
nodes.summary: Local sentence-transformers embedder implementing EmbedderProtocol with lazy model loading, exposing embed_chunks/embed_query and the model dimension.

nodes.name: AzureOpenAIEmbedder
nodes.summary: Azure OpenAI embedder implementing EmbedderProtocol via the optional openai SDK, batching inputs and reading credentials from environment only.

nodes.name: _noun_chunks
nodes.summary: Returns multi-word noun-phrase candidates from text using spaCy when available, gated behind a lazy optional import.

nodes.name: extract_prose
nodes.summary: Extracts deduplicated EntityRecords from a section's prose using several regex patterns plus noun chunks, tagged with the source section id.

nodes.name: build_prose_inventory
nodes.summary: Builds a per-section prose entity inventory for a whole document by iterating its sections and calling extract_prose.

nodes.name: _summarize_table_for_embed
nodes.summary: Condenses a markdown data table into a header row plus a '(table, N data rows)' summary so high-entropy cells do not dominate the embedding vector.

nodes.name: Section
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing one document section: level, title, breadcrumb, content blocks, subsections, and enrichment fields (section_type, entities, tags, depends_on, exposes).

nodes.name: DocumentModel
nodes.summary: Top-level document container holding doc_id, metadata, and the root section tree, with iter_sections to traverse all sections breadth-first.

nodes.name: SemanticChunk
nodes.summary: The pipeline's core embeddable record carrying content plus provenance and enrichment, with to_embed_text (structured header + content), to_metadata (scalar-only), and to_dict serializers.

nodes.name: EntityRecord
nodes.summary: Frozen record for an extracted entity with text, target field, source, confidence, section id, and a normalized canonical form set in __post_init__.

nodes.name: SemanticPipeline
nodes.summary: Orchestrator wiring parse -> enrich -> chunk -> gate -> embed -> store -> search, with lazy embedder/store construction, chunk-quality enforcement, provenance verification, and dense/hybrid retrieval.

nodes.name: MarkdownQualityReport
nodes.summary: Report aggregating QualityIssues from a raw-markdown lint, exposing is_embeddable and an issue_summary.

nodes.name: ChunkQualityReport
nodes.summary: Report for the binding chunk hygiene gate, distinguishing clean chunks from poisoned ones (is_clean / poison).

nodes.name: check_markdown
nodes.summary: Report-only linter over raw source markdown that runs HTML-leakage, Confluence-artifact, code-ratio, link-density, content-density, and orphaned-heading checks.

nodes.name: _strip_code_preserving_lines
nodes.summary: Blanks fenced, indented, and inline code while preserving line numbers, so prose residue checks do not false-positive on code examples.

nodes.name: check_chunk
nodes.summary: The binding chunk hygiene gate: runs structural-absence residue scans on a chunk's de-fenced embed text and flags over-budget/hard-cap chunks as weak.

nodes.name: _scan_residue
nodes.summary: Scans text for markup-shape residue (known/namespace/markup tags, Confluence macros, unrendered entities, base64 blobs) and returns QualityIssues.

nodes.name: _check_orphaned_headings
nodes.summary: Flags headings that have no following body content (empty section headings) by comparing de-fenced and raw line streams.

nodes.name: format_preview
nodes.summary: Builds a Rich Text preview of a search result, rendering breadcrumb, metadata bits, and content for the TUI preview pane.

nodes.name: SearchApp
nodes.summary: Textual App driving the interactive search UI: composes widgets, handles input/switch/select events, runs pipeline.search off-thread, and fills the result table and preview pane.

nodes.name: SearchResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass for a single search hit carrying chunk_id, content, metadata, distance, and optional fused_score, with a similarity score property.

nodes.name: ChromaVectorStore
nodes.summary: ChromaDB-backed VectorStoreProtocol implementation handling upsert, filtered search, corpus retrieval, deletion, reset, and provenance on the collection metadata.

nodes.name: MemoryVectorStore
nodes.summary: In-memory VectorStoreProtocol implementation over langchain-core's InMemoryVectorStore, persisting to a JSON file (atomic writes) with a provenance sidecar and precomputed-vector ingestion.

nodes.name: make_vector_store
nodes.summary: Factory selecting and constructing the vector-store backend (memory or chroma) from config.vector_store_backend.

nodes.name: load_vocabulary
nodes.summary: Loads the cross-corpus entity vocabulary list from a JSON file, returning an empty list when the path is unset or missing.

nodes.name: save_vocabulary
nodes.summary: Persists a sorted, deduplicated entity vocabulary list to a JSON file, creating parent directories as needed.

nodes.name: ast_parser.py
nodes.summary: Thin subprocess wrapper around pandoc that converts Markdown files into pandoc JSON ASTs; the only module in flow A that invokes pandoc.
nodes.languageNotes: Pandoc subprocess decoding is pinned to UTF-8 to avoid Windows cp1252 crashes on non-ASCII content.

nodes.name: generate_ast
nodes.summary: Runs pandoc as a subprocess to convert a single Markdown file to a parsed pandoc JSON AST dict.

nodes.name: generate_ast_batch
nodes.summary: Batch-converts multiple Markdown files to pandoc ASTs, optionally writing each as a JSON artifact to an output directory.

nodes.name: pandoc_version
nodes.summary: Returns the installed pandoc version string by invoking the binary, used for environment checks.

nodes.name: corpus_taxonomy.py
nodes.summary: Builds a cross-document field taxonomy and vocabulary by parsing pipe tables across a corpus and gating fields by minimum document frequency.

nodes.name: build_corpus_taxonomy
nodes.summary: Parses every document in a directory, extracts pipe-table fields per section, and gates them to fields shared by at least min_docs documents.

nodes.name: direction.py
nodes.summary: Loads and resolves per-field directional metadata (e.g. depends_on / exposes) from a YAML config, normalising field names via header normalisation.

nodes.name: load_field_directions
nodes.summary: Reads a YAML field-direction config and returns a mapping of normalised field names to their direction labels.

nodes.name: doc_router.py
nodes.summary: Routes a document to a taxonomy by fingerprinting its heading keys against known doc types (e.g. SAD) above a match threshold.

nodes.name: detect_doc_type
nodes.summary: Classifies a document type by comparing its normalised heading fingerprint against known doc-type signatures above a threshold.

nodes.name: load_taxonomy
nodes.summary: Loads a taxonomy JSON file from disk, returning an empty taxonomy with a warning when the file is missing.

nodes.name: taxonomy_for
nodes.summary: Returns the matching taxonomy for a document by routing on its detected doc type, else an empty taxonomy.

nodes.name: extract_structural.py
nodes.summary: Extracts structured entity records from a document's pipe tables, keyed by section, distinguishing wide tables from key-value tables.

nodes.name: _records_from_table
nodes.summary: Parses a pipe table into EntityRecords, emitting one record per cell for wide tables or a field/value pair for key-value tables while skipping placeholders.

nodes.name: header_norm.py
nodes.summary: Normalises heading and field labels to a canonical lowercase singular form so variant spellings collapse to one taxonomy key.

nodes.name: normalise_header
nodes.summary: Lowercases, strips parenthetical/whitespace noise, and singularises each token of a header to produce a stable canonical key.

nodes.name: structural.py
nodes.summary: Converts a pandoc JSON AST into a DocumentModel section tree using panflute, serialising inlines, lists, and tables and synthesising a root section for preamble content.
nodes.languageNotes: Deterministic panflute traversal; content before the first heading is attached to a synthesized title-derived root so a page never yields zero chunks.

nodes.name: _extract_metadata
nodes.summary: Reads frontmatter/meta keys from the pandoc document into a DocumentMetadata record, parsing labels and singular author key.

nodes.name: _serialize_inline_list
nodes.summary: Recursively serialises a list of panflute inline elements (text, emphasis, code, links) into a plain-text string with link hints.

nodes.name: _serialize_list
nodes.summary: Serialises ordered/bullet lists into indented Markdown-style text, recursing into nested lists and handling embedded blocks.

nodes.name: _serialize_table
nodes.summary: Serialises a panflute table into a pipe-delimited text block, normalising cell line breaks and padding rows to a consistent column count.

nodes.name: _elem_to_content_block
nodes.summary: Dispatches a panflute block element to the correct serialiser and wraps the result in a typed ContentBlock with a stable hash id.

nodes.name: build_structural_model
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that walks the pandoc AST building a nested Section tree and DocumentModel, synthesising a root section for heading-less preamble content.

nodes.name: template_taxonomy.py
nodes.summary: Extracts a canonical field taxonomy from a single template Markdown file by parsing its pipe tables and section headings, with a CLI entry point.

nodes.name: parse_pipe_table
nodes.summary: Parses a Markdown pipe table into header and body rows, splitting cells, unescaping pipes, and discarding separator lines.

nodes.name: fields_and_orientation
nodes.summary: Determines a table's orientation (wide vs key-value) and returns the deduplicated, normalised field names it defines.

nodes.name: extract_taxonomy
nodes.summary: Parses a template Markdown file into a structural model and walks its sections to build a per-section field taxonomy.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that extracts a taxonomy from a template path and writes the canonical JSON to an output file.

nodes.name: dump.py
nodes.summary: Model-free debugging entry point that runs pandoc -> structural -> enrich -> chunk for a single markdown file and writes the AST, enriched model, and chunks as JSON artifacts under the workspace outbox.
nodes.languageNotes: Run as `python -m sdd_pipeline.dump`; loads no embedding model so it works without the ML model download.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI driver that resolves input/output paths under the workspace contract, then dumps the pandoc AST, enriched document model, and semantic chunks to JSON files.

nodes.name: enrichment.py
nodes.summary: Rule-based enrichment stage that classifies section types, extracts entities and tags, applies inventory/direction-driven field writes, and scans a corpus to discover a cross-document entity vocabulary.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure stdlib `re` with precompiled patterns; deterministic and unit-testable per the architecture guardrails.

nodes.name: classify_section_type
nodes.summary: Maps a section title to a SectionType by matching ordered keyword rules (first match wins).

nodes.name: extract_entities
nodes.summary: Extracts domain entities from section text via service/tech/protocol regex patterns plus optional injected project vocabulary, returning a sorted canonical list.

nodes.name: extract_tags
nodes.summary: Derives content tags from a section's title, classified type, and code-fence languages for use in the chunk embed header.

nodes.name: enrich_section
nodes.summary: Recursively enriches a section in place: classifies its type, computes entities and tags, and applies any matching inventory records before recursing into children.

nodes.name: enrich_document
nodes.summary: Top-level enrichment entry that loads field directions and enriches every root section of a DocumentModel with entity terms and inventory data.

nodes.name: _write_to_field
nodes.summary: Routes a reconciled entity record into the appropriate section field (entities, tags, or metadata) based on its resolved direction.

nodes.name: _apply_inventory
nodes.summary: Reconciles a section's inventory records by confidence threshold and writes the surviving records into the section's fields.

nodes.name: _collect_raw_terms
nodes.summary: Discovery-only term collector that broadens recall using service/tech/protocol plus all-caps and backtick patterns for the corpus scan.

nodes.name: scan_corpus
nodes.summary: Scans a set of documents seeded with known terms to build a cross-corpus entity vocabulary so a term seen in one doc is recognized in all.

nodes.name: reconcile.py
nodes.summary: Deduplicates EntityRecords by canonical key, keeping the highest-confidence/superseding record per entity for enrichment.

nodes.name: reconcile
nodes.summary: Folds a list of EntityRecords into the best record per canonical key (case-folded), resolving conflicts via the supersedes rule and returning a stable sorted result.

nodes.name: _supersedes
nodes.summary: Decides whether a candidate EntityRecord should replace the current incumbent based on confidence and tie-breaking criteria.

nodes.name: workspace.py
nodes.summary: Workspace path-contract helpers that confine CLI inputs to the inbox and outputs to the outbox, resolving/validating paths and creating directories with containment enforcement.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pathlib containment checks (`is_relative_to` + case-folded prefix) to prevent path traversal outside the workspace roots.

nodes.name: WorkspaceError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when a user-supplied path violates the inbox/outbox workspace containment contract.

nodes.name: resolve_input
nodes.summary: Resolves a user input path against the inbox directory, enforcing containment unless the workspace contract is disabled.

nodes.name: resolve_output_dir
nodes.summary: Resolves and optionally creates an output directory under the outbox, enforcing workspace containment.

nodes.name: resolve_output_file
nodes.summary: Resolves an output file path under the outbox, enforcing containment and optionally creating its parent directory.

nodes.name: resolve_index_path
nodes.summary: Resolves the vector-index path under the outbox, applying the workspace containment contract.

nodes.name: ensure_inbox_exists
nodes.summary: Verifies the inbox directory exists when the workspace contract is enforced, raising WorkspaceError otherwise.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the HTML-to-GitLab-Markdown converter (flow B), re-exporting the public surface (convert_file, postprocess, resolve_pandoc, stats, ConversionError, ConversionNotes) so importing the package never pulls in the indexing pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using explicit __all__ to define the public surface; imports only from convert/base.py and convert/html_to_gitlab_md.py.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Engine-agnostic shared layer for the converter: pandoc resolution/invocation, fence-aware markdown post-processing, YAML-safe frontmatter emission, and document statistics, reusable by both the HTML and the planned docx conversion paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Holds the ConversionError/ConversionNotes contracts plus the text-layer helpers; uses fence-aware regex so code examples are not mangled by prose fixes.

nodes.name: ConversionError
nodes.summary: Exception raised (rather than sys.exit) when conversion fails, so batch callers can collect per-file failures.

nodes.name: ConversionNotes
nodes.summary: Mutable report accumulator carrying warnings, errors, macro counts, detected languages, and page metadata gathered during a conversion run; serializes to a dict for the convert report.

nodes.name: resolve_pandoc
nodes.summary: Resolves the pandoc executable path (explicit override or PATH lookup via shutil.which), raising ConversionError when pandoc is unavailable.

nodes.name: _run_pandoc
nodes.summary: Runs pandoc as a subprocess with UTF-8-pinned encoding, returning decoded stdout and raising ConversionError on non-zero exit.

nodes.name: _apply_outside_fences
nodes.summary: Applies a transform function only to prose segments of markdown, preserving fenced code blocks verbatim so code examples are not rewritten.

nodes.name: _dump_frontmatter
nodes.summary: Serializes a frontmatter mapping to YAML-safe output, sanitizing string values and warning via notes when dumping fails.

nodes.name: postprocess
nodes.summary: Cleans pandoc-emitted GitLab markdown with fence-aware prose fixes, optionally prepends YAML frontmatter (title/author/space/source_url/labels/date/page_id) and an opt-in TOC.

nodes.name: stats
nodes.summary: Computes per-document conversion metrics (sections, pictures, code snippets, lists, tables, urls) with legacy key aliases preserved for back-compat.

nodes.name: html_to_gitlab_md.py
nodes.summary: The rendered-HTML conversion path of flow B: BeautifulSoup pre-clean that rewrites Confluence constructs into PFI-HTML, the pandoc html->json->gfm bridge, page-chrome harvesting, a storage-format front door, and the convert_file public entry plus a CLI.
nodes.languageNotes: Large single-module handler suite (~50 normalisation functions) built on BeautifulSoup; storage-format input is refused up front via _reject_if_storage_format rather than silently mangled.

nodes.name: detect_confluence_version
nodes.summary: Sniffs the Confluence export version from soup markers, recorded into notes.metadata to feed the converter confidence gate.

nodes.name: preprocess
nodes.summary: BeautifulSoup pre-clean orchestrator: harvests page chrome, finds the content root, and runs the full chain of Confluence-construct normalisers (admonitions, expands, ADR cards, badges, jira, tasks, code, layouts, tables) into PFI-HTML for pandoc.
nodes.languageNotes: Central pipeline that sequences ~30 normalisation handlers; records confluence_version and root_fallback signals consumed by the CLI confidence gate.

nodes.name: _harvest_page_chrome
nodes.summary: Extracts page title, space, author, date, page_id, and labels from the export chrome into notes.metadata before content-root selection, feeding the frontmatter.

nodes.name: _normalise_inline_text
nodes.summary: Normalises inline text carriers (status spans, time elements) by unwrapping or replacing them with plain text.

nodes.name: _find_content_root
nodes.summary: Selects the main content container from the export, falling back to <body> and flagging root_fallback in notes so the confidence gate can quarantine low-confidence pages.

nodes.name: _remove_ui_chrome
nodes.summary: Strips Confluence UI chrome (navigation, controls, decorations) from the content tree before conversion.

nodes.name: _normalise_admonitions
nodes.summary: Rewrites Confluence admonition macros (info/note/warning panels, including table-shaped variants) into labelled blockquote carriers for pandoc.

nodes.name: _normalise_expands
nodes.summary: Converts Confluence expand macros into a bold-label paragraph followed by the expand body (no <details> element).

nodes.name: _flatten_adr_cards
nodes.summary: Flattens ADR/decision-record card constructs into a heading plus body nodes so design-decision content survives conversion as plain markdown.

nodes.name: _normalise_badges
nodes.summary: Rewrites Confluence status lozenges/badges into bold inline text.

nodes.name: _normalise_jira
nodes.summary: Converts Jira issue macros/links into plain text or link references for the markdown output.

nodes.name: _normalise_emoticons
nodes.summary: Maps Confluence emoticon markup to unicode/text equivalents keyed by the emoticon name.

nodes.name: _normalise_task_lists
nodes.summary: Rewrites Confluence task-list constructs into GitLab-flavoured markdown checkbox list items.

nodes.name: _flatten_samepage_links
nodes.summary: Flattens same-page anchor links into plain text to avoid dangling fragment references in the exported markdown.

nodes.name: _normalise_layouts
nodes.summary: Flattens Confluence multi-column layout sections into a single content flow with no horizontal-rule separators.

nodes.name: _normalise_ac_links
nodes.summary: Legacy storage-format handler for <ac:link> elements; unreachable from convert_file (the front door rejects storage input) and kept only for direct unit tests.

nodes.name: _normalise_ac_images
nodes.summary: Legacy storage-format handler for <ac:image> elements, retained for unit tests only since storage input is rejected by the front door.

nodes.name: _drop_leftover_ac
nodes.summary: Removes any leftover Atlassian (ac:/ri:/at:) tags that survived earlier normalisation, bumping macro counts in notes.

nodes.name: _normalise_diagrams
nodes.summary: Handles diagram macros (draw.io/gliffy and similar), optionally keeping them or replacing with caption paragraphs depending on the keep_diagrams flag.

nodes.name: _normalise_gallery
nodes.summary: Normalises Confluence image-gallery macros into a flat sequence of images/captions.

nodes.name: _normalise_viewfiles
nodes.summary: Rewrites Confluence view-file/attachment-preview macros into plain links or captions.

nodes.name: _detect_language
nodes.summary: Infers a code block's language from existing class hints or heuristic content sniffing for fenced-block annotation.

nodes.name: _normalise_code_blocks
nodes.summary: Rebuilds Confluence code macros and SyntaxHighlighter blocks into clean <pre> elements with detected language so pandoc emits proper fenced code.

nodes.name: convert
nodes.summary: Drives the pandoc html->json->gfm bridge over pre-cleaned HTML, applying the in-process panflute filter and returning the gfm markdown.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Validates the produced markdown for residual conversion artefacts, optionally reporting verbosely.

nodes.name: _reject_if_storage_format
nodes.summary: Front door that sniffs for literal <ac:/<ri:/<at: storage-format tags and refuses such input with ConversionError rather than mangling it.

nodes.name: convert_file
nodes.summary: Public entry point converting one rendered-HTML export to Markdown end to end: reject storage input, preprocess, pandoc-bridge, postprocess with frontmatter, and return (out_path, markdown, metrics, notes).

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse parser for the legacy single-file CLI entry of the HTML converter.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Legacy standalone CLI entry point that converts a single HTML file and prints a report (emits emoji; run with PYTHONUTF8=1 on Windows).

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template of runtime environment variables (PIPELINE_* prefix) documenting the pandoc format, embedding model/provider, chunk size, vector-store backend, Chroma persist dir, and collection name used to configure PipelineConfig.
nodes.languageNotes: Loaded via pydantic-settings as PipelineConfig; every key uses the PIPELINE_ prefix except ANONYMIZED_TELEMETRY (Chroma).

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project guidance for Claude Code covering commands, the two-flow architecture (indexing/search pipeline and HTMLâ†’GitLab-MD converter), module boundaries, known pitfalls, configuration, embedding providers, and vector-store backends.
nodes.languageNotes: Authoritative architecture reference with per-module role tables and guardrails enforcing module separation.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project documentation: quick start, workspace contract, full CLI reference (index/search/convert/export/scan/lint/check), pipeline stages, embedding providers, vector-store backends, environment setup, and Python API examples.

nodes.name: devfile.yaml
nodes.summary: Eclipse Che / OpenShift Dev Spaces devfile defining the remote development container, components, and named run-task commands for building and exercising the pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Devfile v2 schema: components provision the workspace container; commands surface as Command Palette run tasks.

nodes.name: environment.yml
nodes.summary: Conda/micromamba environment specification pinning the Python version, channels, and dependencies needed to run the pipeline (including pandoc) on Windows.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python package definition and tooling config: dependencies, optional extras (dev/chroma/azure/tui), the sdd-pipeline console script entry point, plus pytest, coverage, ruff, and mypy settings.
nodes.languageNotes: setuptools build backend; pytest sets pythonpath=src so imports resolve without install; ruff line-length 100 / py311 target; coverage gate fail_under=70.

nodes.name: entity-vocab.json
nodes.summary: Committed seed cross-corpus entity vocabulary (a JSON array of project domain terms like KPO, XCom, triggerer) loaded by vocabulary.py and merged into the two-pass corpus scan so these terms are recognised as entities across all documents.
nodes.languageNotes: A flat JSON string array (not an object); load_vocabulary sorts and dedupes it on read.

nodes.name: field_directions.yaml
nodes.summary: Dependency-direction map that routes a document table's field name (after header_norm normalisation) to either Section.exposes or Section.depends_on during enrichment; loaded by direction.load_field_directions and seeded from the eval-corpus field vocabulary.
nodes.languageNotes: Two top-level keys (exposes / depends_on), each a list of natural-language field names; matching is name-based, never inferred from column position.

nodes.name: taxonomy.json
nodes.summary: Committed SAD-template taxonomy mapping each known section heading (definitions, quality attribute, solution components, stakeholder) to its canonical field list and table orientation (wide vs key_value); loaded by doc_router.load_taxonomy to drive structural inventory extraction.
nodes.languageNotes: Canonical JSON keyed by normalised section heading; each entry carries fields[] and an orientation discriminator consumed by the inventory extractors.

nodes.name: confluence-storage-format-gitlab-markdown.md
nodes.summary: Superseded reference note on converting Confluence storage format to GitLab Markdown, kept for history after confluence-conversion-rules.md replaced it.

nodes.name: convert-html-gitlab-markdown-linux.md
nodes.summary: Linux operator instructions for running the HTML-to-GitLab-Markdown converter, covering setup and invocation steps.

nodes.name: convert-html-gitlab-markdown-windows.md
nodes.summary: Windows operator instructions for running the HTML-to-GitLab-Markdown converter, covering setup and invocation steps.

nodes.name: index.md
nodes.summary: Wiki index page that catalogs all guide pages and the learning knowledge base, with per-page summaries and last-updated dates serving as the documentation entry point.

nodes.name: log.md
nodes.summary: Chronological wiki ingestion log recording each source-to-wiki-page mapping and feature/refactor event in the documentation workflow.

nodes.name: pipeline-101.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive operator runbook covering the seven-stage indexing pipeline: onboarding a document to chunks, incremental updates without a full re-index, vocabulary/taxonomy management, semantic-quality evaluation, and a troubleshooting loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Long-form step-by-step guide organized into seven numbered sections plus quick-reference and cheat-sheet tables.

nodes.name: CONFLUENCE_FORMAT_INSTRUCTIONS.md

nodes.name: 01-trace-a-document.md

nodes.name: 03-parametrize-header-norm.md

nodes.name: 04-add-a-config-field.md

nodes.name: 01-models-and-config.md

nodes.name: 02-ast-parser-and-structural.md
nodes.summary: Guided tour of stages 2-4: how ast_parser.py shells out to pandoc for a JSON AST and structural.py turns that flat header sequence into a typed DocumentModel tree via a level-keyed stack.
nodes.languageNotes: Explains the tagged-union pandoc node shape and the stack-based outline-building algorithm with a real AST excerpt.

nodes.name: 03-enrichment.md
nodes.summary: Guided tour of stage 5 enrichment: the ordered keyword rules for section-type classification, the three precision entity-extraction regexes, and the optional inventory routing into depends_on/exposes.
nodes.languageNotes: Emphasizes determinism (no ML) and the precision-vs-recall asymmetry between extract_entities and scan_corpus.

nodes.name: 04-vocabulary-and-two-pass-scan.md
nodes.summary: Guided tour of the cross-corpus vocabulary: vocabulary.py I/O plus the two-pass scan flow (scan_corpus discovery then enrich-with-full-list) that lets a term coined in one document be recognized in all.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes a Mermaid sequence diagram of the two-pass index_directory flow and documents the scan/index/export CLI surface.

nodes.name: 05-taxonomy-modules.md
nodes.summary: Guided tour of the four taxonomy modules (header_norm, template_taxonomy, corpus_taxonomy, doc_router) that determine which fields a SAD template defines per section so live document tables can be matched against them.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers header normalisation as spec, wide vs key_value table orientation, document-frequency gating, and SAD fingerprint routing with real chunk-count payoffs.

nodes.name: 06-inventory-extraction-modules.md
nodes.summary: Guided tour of stage 3.5 inventory extraction: how extract_structural, extract_prose, direction, and reconcile mine per-section EntityRecords from tables and prose and route them by field name into depends_on/exposes/metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents the confidence ladder (table 1.0 > prose 0.5-0.9), name-not-position routing, and the deterministic reconcile tie-break with a real depends_on payoff.

nodes.name: 07-chunking.md
nodes.summary: Guided tour of stage 6 chunking: how chunk_document flattens the enriched section tree into SemanticChunks, the three merge strategies, the split cascade, dual char budgets, and per-chunk entity scoping.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes Mermaid flowcharts for merge strategies and explains entity_fn dependency injection and deterministic chunk-id construction.

nodes.name: 08-embeddings-and-vector-store.md
nodes.summary: Guided tour of stage 7: the EmbedderProtocol and VectorStoreProtocol seams, lazy model loading, memory vs Chroma backends, precomputed-vector writes, and the provenance check that refuses to search a foreign index.
nodes.languageNotes: Frames Python Protocols as structural C# interfaces; covers lazy loading, atomic persistence, and provenance verification.

nodes.name: 09-retrieval-and-hybrid-search.md
nodes.summary: Guided tour of hybrid retrieval: retrieval.py's Okapi BM25 and Reciprocal Rank Fusion, plus pipeline._hybrid_search step by step (over-fetch dense pool, BM25 over get_corpus, fuse, map back).
nodes.languageNotes: Explains the BM25+ idf floor, the rrf_k weighting trade-off, and why fused scores are ranks not similarities, with a Mermaid fusion flowchart.

nodes.name: 10-pipeline-orchestrator-and-cli.md
nodes.summary: Guided tour of the composition root: how SemanticPipeline wires the seven stages method by method and how cli.py's eight typer commands map onto pipeline calls, including the config-override pattern.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses method-and-command summary tables and explains lazy seams, the -1 error sentinel, and the --backend None override subtlety.

nodes.name: 11-quality-linter.md

nodes.languageNotes: Frames check_markdown as a Roslyn-style analyzer; contrasts the advisory raw-source linter with the post-pipeline check_chunk gate.

layers.name: Data Contracts & Configuration

layers.description: Deterministic Confluence-MD to SemanticChunk stages: pandoc AST parse, structural section tree, enrichment/taxonomy tagging, inventory extraction, header normalization, vocabulary scan, reconciliation, routing, and chunking.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to learn what sdd-pipeline is: a semantic-search pipeline that turns Confluence-exported Software Design Documents into a vector-search index, plus a second, independent flow that converts Confluence HTML into GitLab Markdown. The README lays out the quick-start, the CLI reference (index/search/convert/export/scan/lint/check), and the seven pipeline stages — giving you the roadmap this tour will follow stage by stage.

tour.title: The CLI Front Door
tour.description: cli.py is the project's entry point — the Typer app exposed as the `sdd-pipeline` console script (wired in pyproject.toml). It defines every user-facing command (index, search, convert, export, scan, lint, check, tui) and dispatches each to the pipeline or converter layers. Reading it first shows you, from the outside in, every capability the codebase offers before you dive into how each is implemented.
tour.languageLesson: Typer builds a CLI from type-annotated Python functions: each command is a decorated function whose parameters become flags/arguments, and the console-script entry point in pyproject.toml (`sdd-pipeline = sdd_pipeline.cli:app`) is how `pip install` exposes it on PATH.

tour.title: Data Contracts
tour.description: models.py is the most depended-upon module in the project (highest fan-in). It holds pure data contracts with no service logic: the structural types (ContentBlock, Section, DocumentModel) that flow through the pipeline, the central SemanticChunk record with its embed-text/metadata serializers, and the SectionType enum. Every later stage speaks this vocabulary, so understanding these shapes makes the rest of the tour click into place.
tour.languageLesson: Python dataclasses give you typed, immutable-friendly record types with auto-generated constructors — similar to C# records. Keeping them dependency-free (no service logic) makes them safe to import everywhere without circular dependencies.

tour.title: Configuration
tour.description: config.py defines PipelineConfig, the pydantic-settings model that loads every tunable (embedding model, vector-store backend, chunk budgets, hybrid-search knobs, robustness gates) from PIPELINE_-prefixed environment variables or a .env file. Committed seed files like taxonomy.json supply the SAD-template section-to-field mappings that the enrichment stage relies on. Together they are the configuration substrate both flows share.
tour.languageLesson: pydantic-settings validates and coerces env vars into a typed config object at startup, so misconfiguration fails fast and loud rather than surfacing as a confusing runtime error deep in the pipeline.

tour.title: The Orchestrator
tour.description: pipeline.py is the hub of Flow A: SemanticPipeline wires the seven stages together, lazily constructing the embedder and vector store on first use (so unit tests can inject mocks), enforcing the chunk-quality gate, and verifying index provenance. Its imports fan out to every stage module — this is the natural reading order the rest of the indexing tour follows. Building on the CLI from Step 2, this is where a command like `index` actually executes.
tour.languageLesson: Lazy attribute construction (building the embedder/store only on first access) keeps expensive side effects out of object creation, which is what lets the test suite swap in lightweight fakes without ever loading a real model.

tour.title: Stages 1-2: Pandoc AST and Structural Model
tour.description: The first two indexing stages turn raw markdown into a structured tree. ast_parser.py is the only module that invokes pandoc — a thin subprocess wrapper producing a pandoc JSON AST. structural.py then walks that AST with panflute into a DocumentModel section tree (from Step 3), synthesizing a root section for any preamble content so a heading-less page never yields zero chunks. Both are deterministic and unit-testable by design.

tour.title: Stage 3: Semantic Enrichment
tour.description: enrichment.py is a rule-based stage (high fan-in) that classifies each section's SectionType, extracts entities and tags, applies inventory/direction-driven field writes from the taxonomy, and can scan a whole corpus to discover a cross-document entity vocabulary. This is where the deterministic 'meaning' is attached to the structural tree — the tags and entities that later sharpen search filtering and embed-text quality.

tour.title: Stage 4: Chunking
tour.description: chunking.py converts the enriched DocumentModel into a list of SemanticChunks — the units that get embedded. It handles prose/definition merging, embed-budget-aware text splitting, and per-chunk entity scoping so a term mentioned once doesn't bleed across sibling chunks. The embed-text format (structural context prepended to content) defined here is what gives the vectors their quality.
tour.languageLesson: The recursive section walk uses nested closures (emit/flush) to buffer prose as it descends the tree — a common Python pattern for accumulating state across a traversal without threading it through every call signature.

tour.title: The Quality Gate
tour.description: quality.py is stdlib-only and serves two roles: check_markdown is a report-only linter over raw source markdown (the `lint` command), while check_chunk is the binding hygiene gate wired into the pipeline. The gate is a structural-absence check — after de-fencing, prose must contain no markup shape — so poisoned chunks block the file while merely oversized ones only warn. It is the project's guarantee that embedding-harmful residue never reaches the index.

tour.title: Stages 5-6: Embeddings and Vector Store
tour.description: embeddings.py is the only module that loads an embedding backend, offering a local sentence-transformers embedder and an Azure OpenAI one behind a shared EmbedderProtocol. vector_store.py is the only module touching a vector-store backend, with pluggable in-memory (langchain-core) and Chroma implementations behind VectorStoreProtocol. Both use the protocol-plus-factory pattern so the pipeline and tests depend only on the interface, never a concrete backend.
tour.languageLesson: Python's Protocol enables structural typing (duck typing with static checks): make_embedder/make_vector_store return whichever concrete implementation the config selects, and callers stay decoupled from the choice — the same dependency-inversion idea as a C# interface, but without explicit `implements`.

tour.title: Stage 7: Retrieval and Hybrid Search
tour.description: retrieval.py adds the lexical half of search: a pure-Python BM25 index fused with the dense vector ranking via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF). This complements semantic similarity with a deterministic keyword signal, so exact-term queries aren't lost in vector space. Combined with the vector store from Step 10, this completes the index-to-answer path that the `search --hybrid` command drives.

tour.title: Flow B: HTML to GitLab Markdown Converter
tour.description: The convert/ subpackage is a second, fully independent flow that turns Confluence HTML exports into GitLab Markdown — importing it never pulls in Flow A. base.py holds the engine-agnostic shared layer (pandoc invocation, fence-aware post-processing, YAML frontmatter); html_to_gitlab_md.py is the BeautifulSoup pre-clean and conversion path; confluence_pf_filter.py is the in-process panflute filter that rewrites admonitions, expands, and tables. This is the upstream that produces clean markdown for Flow A to index.

tour.title: The Search TUI
tour.description: tui.py is a thin Textual presentation layer over SemanticPipeline that keeps the embedder and index warm across queries — a live query box, section/space filters, a hybrid toggle, and a result/preview pane. It imports Textual only when the command runs, so core flows never depend on it, and runs the blocking search on a worker thread to keep the UI responsive. It is the interactive face of everything built in Steps 5-11.

tour.title: Tests and the Model-Free Contract
tour.description: conftest.py anchors the test strategy: it provides workspace isolation, sample markdown/AST/DocumentModel/chunk fixtures, and a deterministic, model-free hashing_embedder that exercises the real index-to-search path without downloading a 1.3 GB model or needing pandoc. This is how the fast unit lane validates the search contract (filters narrow, output is stable, hybrid runs) while the slow end-to-end tests cover true semantic relevance. The pipeline-101 guide ties the whole design back together.
tour.languageLesson: pytest fixtures are dependency-injected by name: a test simply lists `hashing_embedder` as a parameter and pytest supplies it. This is what lets the suite swap a deterministic fake for a real ML model so the fast lane stays offline and reproducible.
